export const products = [ { id: 1, name: "Cerveza Modelo", price: 919.11, image: "/assets/img/1.png", }, { id: 2, name: "Diesel Life", price: 1257.92, image: "/assets/img/2.png", }, { id: 3, name: "Indian Cricket Team jersey", price: 1500.85, image: "/assets/img/3.png", }, { id: 4, name: "One Punch man - OK", price: 1250.9, image: "/assets/img/4.png", }, { id: 5, name: "Hiking jacket", price: 1750.85, image: "/assets/img/5.png", }, { id: 6, name: "Real Heart", price: 3100.61, image: "/assets/img/6.png", }, { id: 7, name: "Fredd - Black and White", price: 1801.1, image: "/assets/img/7.png", }, { id: 8, name: "Star Wars - The Last", price: 1199.99, image: "/assets/img/8.png", }, { id: 9, name: "Yellow Blouse", price: 2395.16, image: "/assets/img/9.png", }, { id: 10, name: "Rick and Morty - Supreme", price: 1243.82, image: "/assets/img/10.png", },
];

The ProductCard component shows the product details for each product.

Note that we would import useContext and CartContext in all the components where we need the data that is stored in the context.

The onClick events in the buttons handle the addToCart and increase functions which we have extracted from the CartContext:

import styled from "styled-components";
import { Link } from "react-router-dom";
import { formatCurrency } from "../utils";
import CartContext from "../Context/Cart/CartContext";
import { useContext } from "react"; const ProductCard = ({ product }) => { // Extract these functions from the CartContext const { addToCart, increase, cartItems, sumItems, itemCount } = useContext(CartContext); //Check whether the product is in the cart or not const isInCart = (product) => { return !!cartItems.find((item) => item.id === product.id); }; return ( <CardWrapper> <ProductImage src={product.image + "?v=" + product.id} alt={product.name} /> <ProductName>{product.name}</ProductName> <ProductCardPrice>{formatCurrency(product.price)}</ProductCardPrice> <ProductCardButtons> {isInCart(product) && ( <ButtonAddMore onClick={() => { increase(product); }} className="btn" > Add More </ButtonAddMore> )} {!isInCart(product) && ( <Button onClick={() => addToCart(product)}>Add to Cart</Button> )} </ProductCardButtons> </CardWrapper> );
};

For the code below, we will extract the state and functions that we need for the Cart component, which are: cartItems, checkout and clearCart.

Then, if there are any items in the cartItems array, render the items as CartItem components to the page:

import CartItem from "../components/CartItem";
import { useContext } from "react";
import CartContext from "../Context/Cart/CartContext";
import styled from "styled-components";
import Checkout from "../components/Checkout";
import { Link } from "react-router-dom"; const Cart = () => { // Extract the functions from the Context const { cartItems, checkout, clearCart } = useContext(CartContext); return ( <> <Heading> <h1> Shopping Cart <span>({cartItems.length})</span> </h1> </Heading> {/* Show the checkout message when the Checkout Button has been clicked */} {checkout && ( <CheckoutMsg> <h4>Thank you for your purchase!</h4> <p> Your order has been placed and will be delivered to you within 24 hours. </p> <Link to="/"> <ShopBtn onClick={clearCart}>Continue Shopping</ShopBtn> </Link> </CheckoutMsg> )} <Layout> <div> { <CartItemWrapper> {/* If cart is empty, display message, and if not, display each cart Item in cart: {cartItems.length} */} {cartItems.length === 0 ? ( <h4 style={{}}>Cart is empty</h4> ) : ( <ul> {cartItems.map((product) => ( <CartItem key={product.id} product={product} /> ))} </ul> )} </CartItemWrapper> } </div> <div> {/* Checkout component */} {cartItems.length > 0 && <Checkout />} </div> </Layout> </> );
};

The CartItem component contains the items that are present in the current state. And, we'll extract some functions from the CartContext, namely: removeFromCart, increase and decrease:

import { useContext } from "react";
import CartContext from "../Context/Cart/CartContext";
import styled from "styled-components";
import { formatCurrency } from "../utils";
import TrashIcon from "/assets/icons/trash-outline.svg";
import Plus from "/assets/icons/add-circle-outline.svg";
import Minus from "/assets/icons/remove-circle-outline.svg"; const CartItem = ({ product }) => { const { removeFromCart, increase, decrease } = useContext(CartContext); return ( <SingleCartItem> <CartImage src={product.image} alt={product.name} /> <div> <h5>{product.name}</h5> <p>{formatCurrency(product.price)}</p> </div> {/* Buttons */} <BtnContainer> <button onClick={() => increase(product)} className="btn btn-primary btn-sm mr-2 mb-1" > <Icon src={Plus} alt="" /> </button> <div> <p>Qty: {product.quantity}</p> </div> {/* Display a minus icon or trash/delete icon based on the quantity of a particular product is in the cart */} {product.quantity > 1 && ( <button onClick={() => decrease(product)} className="btn"> <Icon src={Minus} alt="" /> </button> )} {product.quantity === 1 && ( <button onClick={() => removeFromCart(product)} className="btn"> <Icon src={TrashIcon} alt="" /> </button> )} </BtnContainer> </SingleCartItem> );
};

Adding Cart Management

Now that we can add, remove, and display products, the final thing to do is implement our cart management. We have already initialised the cart as an empty array in CartState.jsx, meaning that once we restart the app, it will revert to being empty.

Now, what we will do is make sure that we load the existing cart from the local storage on component load.

Update the initialState method in CartState.jsx as follows:

const initialState = { cartItems: storage, ...sumItems(storage), checkout: false,
};

Next, we need to define the storage, also in the CartContext.jsx:

//Local Storage
const storage = localStorage.getItem("cartItems") ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("cartItems")) : [];

Finally, in the CartReducer.jsx, we will define Storage:

const Storage = (cartItems) => { localStorage.setItem( "cartItems", JSON.stringify(cartItems.length > 0 ? cartItems : []) );
};

And export the function to calculate the total price of the cart and the total quantity of the cart

export const sumItems = (cartItems) => { Storage(cartItems); let itemCount = cartItems.reduce( (total, product) => total + product.quantity, 0 ); let total = cartItems .reduce((total, product) => total + product.price * product.quantity, 0) .toFixed(2); return { itemCount, total };
};

With this, we've successfully completed the implementation of the Shopping App.

Check out the live demo and the code repository on Github.

Conclusion

And we're done!

In the course of this article, we discussed Context and its use and used React to scaffold the interface of a minimal shopping app. We also used context to move data and methods between multiple components and added its functionality using useReducer and dispatch.

If you found this post useful (and I'm sure you did), do well to share this resource with your friends and co-workers, and follow me for more content. If you have a question or find an error or typo, kindly leave your feedback in the comments section.

Thanks for reading, and happy coding!

item.title: 如何为你的 Monorepo 配置 Babel | How to Configure Babel For Your Monorepo
item.description:
在这篇文章中，当我将 Babel 配置添加到 monorepo 时，请加入我，确保它可以支持包需要的不同配置，并理解 Babel 在这件事上令人困惑的文档。

当你的 monorepo 中有多个包需要 babel 进行构建或测试时，是时候以可维护的方式管理 Babel 配置了。

要求

从需求开始总是一件好事，这样我们就知道我们的目标是什么，并帮助我们专注于该任务。

在我的Pedalboard monorepo 中，我有几个使用 Jest 测试的包。 您可能知道，Jest 使用babel-jest OOTB 来将“现代”代码（ESM 等）转换为 Jest 可以在 NodeJS 下运行的 CJS 兼容代码。

这意味着这些包中的每一个都需要有一个符合其要求的 babel 配置——一个包只需要 env 的预设插件，而另一个还需要在其之上的 React 预设以正确解析 JSX 等等。

虽然有些包需要 babel config，但有些包的配置完全相同，在这个问题上重复当然是不可能的。

所以如果总结一下我们的要求：

  • 可以共享和扩展的顶级 common babel 配置
  • 如果包的配置不需要扩展顶层通用配置，则不需要在包中创建配置文件
  • 从 monorepo 的根目录以及包级别本身进行 Jest 测试工作

我们准备好了吗？ 开始吧 ：）

例如，虽然可以有多种类型的配置（json、js 等），但代码示例与类型不一致。 不太清楚什么是 CLI 参数和什么是配置选项，同一上下文的信息位分散在多个页面中（我们很快就会看到）以及……为什么有这么多定义配置文件的方法？

我希望这篇文章将有助于整理一些东西。

所以我的第一个问题是我应该使用什么配置文件格式？ 应该是.babelrc吗？ 也许.babelrc.json ？ 答案可以在这个页面中找到，但最好从monorepo 的部分开始。
同样在另一个地方，文档建议将babel.config.json用于 monorepos。

我们希望有一个将应用于整个项目的根配置，或者他们称之为“项目范围的配置”。
现在，正如我提到的，Babel 文档推荐babel.config.json 。 他们建议在您不需要复杂的配置逻辑的情况下，幸运的是我的 monorepo 不需要那种复杂性。

我在项目的根级别创建了一个babel.config.json文件，内容如下：

 { "presets" : [ "@babel/preset-env" ]
}

令人震惊，我知道，但这是我需要的最低要求，并且适用于所有包 - babel 的 env 预设。

将此文件放在我们的 monorepo 存储库的基本目录的根目录下，确定它是根配置（如此处所述，以一种非常令人困惑的方式）。 这个事实很快就会派上用场。

笑话

检查我的 Babel 配置是否得到尊重是一个不错的选择。 让我们从项目的根级别尝试一下（monorepo 使用 Yarn 的workspaces foreach跨包运行测试。您可以在此处阅读有关它的更多信息）

yarn test

运行失败并出现以下错误：

 SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module

是的，我们的 babel 配置似乎没有得到尊重。 但为什么？
这些都是我的monorepo中的嵌套包，但是当我从项目的根目录或包本身为它们运行测试时，它找不到配置，因为配置位于顶层。

我们如何告诉 Jest 查找项目树？
简单 - 可以选择使用的transformer器工具，即babel-jest 。 我们进入我们的主要 jest 配置并定义转换器在查找 babel 配置时向上查找，如下所示：

 transform: { '\\. [ jt ] sx?$': [ 'babel-jest' , { rootMode: 'upward' }], } ,

现在运行测试有不同的结果 -
这是为什么？ 好吧，错误并没有给错误留下太多空间：



原文:

In this post join me as I add Babel configuration to a monorepo, making sure that it can sustain different configurations packages need and making sense of Babel’s confusing docs on the matter.

When you have multiple packages in your monorepo that require babel for their building or testing steps, it is time to manage Babel configuration in a maintainable manner.

Requirements

Starting up with the requirements is always a good thing to do, so that we know what we’re aiming for and helps us to stay focused on that task.

In my Pedalboard monorepo I have several packages which are tested using Jest. Jest, as you may know, uses babel-jest OOTB in order to transpile the “modern” code (ESM, etc.) into CJS compatible code which Jest can run under NodeJS.

This means that each of these packages need to have a babel configuration which fits its requirements - one package only needs the preset plugins for env while the other also needs the React preset on top of it to parse JSX correctly, among others.

Although some packages require babel config, some have the same configuration exactly and duplication on this matter is out of the question of course.

So if to sum up our requirements:

  • A top level common babel configuration which can be shared and extended
  • No need to create a configuration file in a package if its configuration does not need to extend the top level common configuration
  • Have Jest test work from the root of the monorepo as well from the package level itself

Are we set? Let’s start :)

Babel Poor Documentation

Babel is a great tool but sadly I find its documentation very confusing. For example, while there can be many types of configuration (json, js etc.) the code examples are not consistent with the types. It is not quite clear what is a CLI param and what is a configuration option, bits of information of the same context are scattered across multiple pages (as we will soon see) and… why are there so many ways to define a configuration file?

I hope that this post will help to put some order into things.

Babel configuration format type in a monorepo

So the first question that I had was what configuration file format should I use? should it be .babelrc? Perhaps .babelrc.json? babel.config.mjs?
The answer can be found in this page, but it would be wise to start at the monorepo’s section.
Also in another place the docs recommend using babel.config.json for monorepos.

We would like to have a root configuration which will be applied for the entire project, or as they call it “Project-wide configuration”.
Now, as I mentioned, Babel docs recommend babel.config.json. They recommend that in cases where you do not need to have complex logic to the configuration, and luckily my monorepo does not need complexity of that sort.

I’ve created a babel.config.json file at the root level of the project, with the following content:

{ "presets": ["@babel/preset-env"]
}

Staggering, I know, but this is the bare minimum I need and something that is applied to all packages - the env preset of babel.

Having this file at the root level of the base directory of our monorepo repository determines that it is root configuration (as described here in a very confusing manner). This fact will come handy soon enough.

Jest

Jest is the testing command that I use to test several of the monorepo packages. It is a good candidate to check whether my Babel configuration is respected. Let’s try it from the root level of the project (the monorepo uses Yarn’s workspaces foreach to run the test across the packages. You can read more about it here)

yarn test

The run fails with the following error:

SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module

Yes, it appears that our babel configuration was not respected. But why?
These are all nested packages within my monorepo, but when I run the test for them, either from the project’s root or from the package itself, it cannot find the configuration, since the configuration is way up the top level.

How do we tell Jest to look up the project tree?
Simple - with an option over the transformer tool used, that is babel-jest. We go to our main jest configuration and define the transformer to look upwards when it looks for babel configuration, like this:

transform: { '\\.[jt]sx?$': ['babel-jest', {rootMode: 'upward'}], },

Running the tests now has a different result -
tests for the @pedalboard/hooks pass, tests for the @pedalboard/eslint-plugin-craftsmanlint pass, tests for the @pedalboard/scripts pass, but… the tests for @pedalboard/components do not pass.

Why is that? Well the error does not leave much room for mistakes:

item.title: 了解如何使用免费的地理定位 API | Find Out How to Use the Free Geolocation API
item.description:
发表于在这篇博文中，我们将了解如何使用 Geolocation API 创建一个简单的基于位置的应用程序。
您只需要一个支持 API 的浏览器就可以了。 在本教程中，我们将向您展示如何使用IP Geolocation API创建一个简单的基于位置的应用程序。 我们还将向您展示如何使用 API 的一些更高级的功能，例如设置自定义准确度级别和实时获取位置更新。
除了标准的纬度和经度坐标外，Geolocation API 还可以提供有关高度、航向和速度的准确信息。 所有这些数据在许多情况下都非常有用。 例如，了解准确的信息可以帮助您确定数据的可靠性。 海拔信息可以与其他数据结合使用，以估计旅行时间或寻找潜在的捷径。 航向和速度信息可以一起用于跟踪一段时间内的运动模式或识别人们不规律驾驶的区域。

穆罕默德·劳万德—— Dec 20 '21 21 年 12 月 20 日



原文:

ipstackapi

Posted on May 20

The Geolocation API is a powerful tool that can be used by developers to create location-based applications. In this blog post, we will take a look at how to use the Geolocation API to create a simple location-based application.
The Geolocation API is a simple way to create location-based applications. All you need is a browser that supports the API and you're good to go. In this tutorial, we'll show you how to use the IP Geolocation API to create a simple location-based application. We'll also show you how to use some of the more advanced features of the API, such as setting custom accuracy levels and getting position updates in real-time.
In addition to the standard latitude and longitude coordinates, the Geolocation API can also provide accurate information, on altitude, heading, and speed. All of this data can be very useful in a number of situations. For example, knowing accurate information can help you determine how reliable the data is. Altitude information can be used in conjunction with other data to estimate travel times or find potential shortcuts. Heading and speed information could be used together to track movement patterns over time or identify areas where people are driving erratically.

Pipat Sampaokit - Dec 26 '21

Mohamad Lawand - Dec 20 '21

item.title: SolidJs 入门 - 初学者指南 | Getting started with SolidJs – A Beginner's Guide
item.description:
拉胡尔·夏尔马

发表于主要特点

  • 对真实 DOM 的细粒度更新
  • 一次渲染的心理模型：你的组件是常规的 JavaScript 函数，运行一次来​​设置你的视图
  • 自动依赖跟踪：访问你的反应状态订阅它
  • 提供现代框架功能，如 JSX、片段、上下文、门户、悬念、流式 SSR、渐进式水合、错误边界和并发渲染。
  • 有两种主要类型的构建块组件和反应原语。

    共有三个核心原语：信号、备忘录和效果。

    • 信号：信号是反应式编程的基石。 他们跟踪价值随时间的变化。
    • 效果：效果用于执行副作用。 它们用于检查任何依赖项更改。 与 react 不同，您不需要手动传递依赖项。 备忘录：备忘录让您在许多反应式计算中有效地使用派生值。

    让我们通过简单的例子来理解。

     import { render } from " solid-js/web " ; import { createSignal , createEffect , createMemo } from " solid-js " ; function Counter () { const [ count , setCount ] = createSignal ( 0 ); const increment = () => setCount (( c ) => c + 1 ); createEffect (() => { console . log ( " Count Changed " , count ()); }); const value = createMemo (() => count () * count ()); console . log ( " Value " , value ()); return ( < div > < h1 > { count () } </ h1 > < button type = "button" onClick = { increment } > Increment </ button > </ div > ); } render (() => < Counter />, document . getElementById ( " app " ) ! );
    • 注意：createSignal 就像 react 中的 useState。 唯一的区别是它返回 2 个函数 getter 和 setter。
      • 没有任何依赖，每次信号变化时都会调用它。
       // Solid createEffect (() => { console . log ( " count changed " , getCount ()); }); // React useEffect (() => { console . log ( " count changed " , count ); }, [ count ]);
      注意：createEffect 就像 react 中的 useEffect。 不需要任何依赖项数组。
      • 注意：createMemo 就像 react 中的 useMemo。 不需要任何依赖项数组。

        在线尝试solid

        本地设置：

         > npx degit solidjs/templates/ts counter-app > cd counter-app > npm i > npm run dev

        谢谢你的阅读😊

        有任何问题或补充吗？ 请发表评论。

        必须阅读如果你还没有
        在Github 、 Twitter 、 LinkedIn 、 Medium和Stackblitz上找我。

克里兹蒂安·莫雷诺 - Apr 20 4月20日



原文:

Rahul Sharma

Posted on May 20

Solidjs is a javascript library that helps you create user interface. ,Instead of using a Virtual DOM, It uses reactive programming under the hood to update the DOM.

Key Features

  • Fine-grained updates to the real DOM
  • Render-once mental model: your components are regular JavaScript functions that run once to set up your view
  • Automatic dependency tracking: accessing your reactive state subscribes to it
  • Provides modern framework features like JSX, fragments, Context, Portals, Suspense, streaming SSR, progressive hydration, Error Boundaries and concurrent rendering.
  • Web component friendly and can author custom elements

There are two main types of building blocks Components and Reactive Primitives.

1. Components: A component is a function that accepts props(input) as an argument and returns a JSX element.

const Hello = (props) => { return <h1>Hello {props.name}</h1>;
}; <Hello name="John" />;

2. Reactive Primitives: A reactive primitive is a value that can be observed and ensures your view stays up to date. There are three core primitives: Signals, Memos, and Effects.

  • Signals: Signals are the building blocks of reactive programming. They track the value change over time.
  • Effects: Effects are used to perform side effects. They are used to check any dependencies changes. Unlike react you don't need to pass dependencies manually. Solid will automatically be tracking its dependencies, and automatically reruns the function whenever the dependencies update.
  • Memos: Memos let you efficiently use a derived value in many reactive computations.

Lets understand with simple example. 

import { render } from "solid-js/web";
import { createSignal, createEffect, createMemo } from "solid-js"; function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = createSignal(0); const increment = () => setCount((c) => c + 1); createEffect(() => { console.log("Count Changed", count()); }); const value = createMemo(() => count() * count()); console.log("Value", value()); return ( <div> <h1>{count()}</h1> <button type="button" onClick={increment}> Increment </button> </div> );
} render(() => <Counter />, document.getElementById("app")!);
  • createSignal: It returns a signal and a function to update the signal. 
// Solid
const [getCount, setCount] = createSignal(0); // React
const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
NOTE: createSignal is like useState in react. Only difference is that it return 2 function getter and setter.
  • createEffect: It accepts a function that will be called when the signal changes. Without any dependencies, it will be called every time the signal changes. 
// Solid
createEffect(() => { console.log("count changed", getCount());
}); // React
useEffect(() => { console.log("count changed", count);
}, [count]);
NOTE: createEffect is like useEffect in react. Does not require any dependencies array.
  • createMemo: createMemo creates a read-only reactive value equal to the return value of the given function and makes sure that the function only gets executed when its dependencies change. 
// Solid
const value = createMemo(() => computeExpensiveValue(a(), b())); // React
const value = useMemo(() => computeExpensiveValue(a(), b()), [a, b]);
NOTE: createMemo is like useMemo in react. Does not require any dependencies array.

Try solid online

Getting started with Solid is to try it online, https://playground.solidjs.com is the easy way to try out ideas.

Local setup:

> npx degit solidjs/templates/ts counter-app
> cd counter-app
> npm i
> npm run dev

Thank you for reading 😊

Got any questions or additional? please leave a comment.

Must Read If you haven't
React best practices and patterns to reduce code
3 steps to create custom state management library with React and Context API
How to cancel Javascript API request with AbortController
13 Typescript Utility: A Cheat Sheet for Developer
More content at Dev.to.
Catch me on Github, Twitter, LinkedIn, Medium, and Stackblitz.

Njiva Olaf { dev-it-out } - May 16

Khriztian Moreno - Apr 20
item.author: devsmitra

item.title: 为什么你应该放弃 Shopify 购买 Medusa，一种开源替代品 | Why You Should Drop Shopify for Medusa, An Open Source Alternative
item.description:
但是，它对企业和开发人员也有限制，随着时间的推移，使用起来很麻烦。

由于其简单的架构和可扩展性，它拥有一个不断发展的社区。

本文探讨了您应该考虑从 Shopify 切换到 Medusa 以用于您的电子商务堆栈的一些原因。

开源解决方案是未来

然而，企业很快意识到这限制了他们，因为他们无法控制或拥有他们的堆栈。 他们没有自由选择他们想要使用的服务或基础设施。

开源解决方案为企业提供了大量优势，其中最显着的就是完全拥有自己的技术堆栈的能力。 企业可以完全访问该软件并对其进行任何更改或添加，而无需等待原始开发人员的许可。 这使企业可以根据自己的需求量身定制软件，使其非常适合他们的业务。

开源解决方案通常由大型开发人员社区开发，他们不断致力于添加新功能和改进软件。 这给企业带来了巨大的优势，因为他们的软件正在不断地由大型社区进行实战测试和改进，而不必由较小的团队手动完成。

这也意味着企业及其开发团队可以在必要时从社区获得帮助和支持。 例如，美杜莎有一个Discord 服务器，任何人都可以几乎立即从社区和美杜莎核心团队获得帮助。

更多定制能力

这导致企业和开发人员采用 hacky 变通办法，这会导致额外的麻烦和时间问题。

另一方面，美杜莎非常适合定制，因为它的架构被设计为灵活和可扩展的。 例如，Medusa 具有基于组件的结构，可以更轻松地更改结构的一部分，而无需推断其余部分等。

因为它是开源的，开发人员可以根据需要轻松更改 Medusa 的核心，并使用Medusa 的 API在其上构建店面或管理面板。

创建为企业量身定制的店面

店面对于电子商务企业来说很重要，因为它们建立了品牌的身份并为客户提供了购买产品的界面。

有很多 Shopify 主题和模板为企业提供有限的自定义功能。 但是，这些定制功能不足以让企业根据其业务创建完全定制的体验。 当企业提供具有不同购买体验的定制产品时，这将成为一个更大的问题。

另一方面，像 Medusa 这样的无头商务替代方案将前端与后端分离。 开发人员可以完全自由地自定义店面的功能和设计，或者使用他们喜欢的任何前端框架，而不会影响后端。 他们所需要的只是使用 REST API 与后端交互。

您可以将这些店面与您的 Medusa 服务器一起使用：一个使用Next.js 构建，一个使用Gatsby构建，另一个使用medusa.express 构建。 您还可以通过连接到 Medusa 的 REST API 来使用您选择的框架创建您的店面。 您的前端开发将更快，因为可以在不影响后端的情况下完成对前端的更新。

更好的开发者体验

使用 Shopify 的开发人员通常最终会构建一整套在 Shopify 上运行的自定义应用程序以实现自定义。 这使得随着时间的推移难以维护并导致高额技术债务。

与其他基础设施复杂的无头电商平台相比，美杜莎的架构更简单、更容易。 一切，包括其核心引擎和所有插件、集成和第三方服务都在单个 Node JS 服务器中运行，使开发人员更容易在不使用连接器和翻译层的情况下合并插件。

此外，企业可以依靠一位开发人员来维护美杜莎，因为它非常简单。 店面、管理面板和后端都使用 JavaScript 和 Typescript。 这使得在一个或另一个工作之间切换变得容易。

此外，由于前端和后端是解耦的，因此您不需要前端和后端都熟练的开发人员。 您可以每人拥有一个。

此外，Medusa 提供了出色的开发人员资源，包括丰富的文档、自动化开发人员面临的冗余任务的CLI ，以及在为 Medusa 构建店面的同时轻松与后端交互的React hooks 库和JavaScript 客户端。

开发人员还可以报告他们在使用 Medusa 时发现的任何问题、建议新功能或打开拉取请求 (PR) 以修复他们在GitHub存储库中发现的问题。

此外，如果开发人员在使用 Medusa 时遇到任何问题，他们可以在Discord服务器上从 Medusa 的核心团队和社区获得帮助。

多币种支持

多币种支持是一项基本功能，企业可以从其电子商务商店中受益匪浅。 它应该允许他们以多种货币进行销售，并完全控制不同货币的产品定价。

如果您使用 Shopify Payments，Shopify 仅提供多币种支持，仅在17 个国家/地区可用。 因此，如果您的商店在不受支持的国家/地区列表中的国家/地区运营，则多币种功能将无法使用。

即使企业可以使用 Shopify 的多币种功能，他们也无法管理每种货币的产品价格，除非他们使用高级计划。

使用 Shopify 的企业经常求助于创建多个商店来管理不同的货币。 但是，这会导致大量人工工作和人为错误，因为他们需要管理不同商店中的产品、订单和配置。

借助美杜莎，企业可以选择无限数量的销售地区，选择哪些国家属于某个地区，每个地区使用什么货币，并在一个地方为每种货币设置产品价格.

这消除了对企业和开发人员施加的任何限制以及额外的冗余工作的需要。 您仍然可以从一个地方管理您的所有产品、订单和商店配置，同时支持多种货币。

推荐阅读： Medusa 如何解决 Shopify 的多币种问题

全渠道支持

简而言之，全渠道支持意味着跨渠道的统一体验。 例如，用户可以浏览和购买具有相似体验的产品，无论他们是在网站上还是在移动应用程序上。

想要利用全渠道的企业要么需要 Shopify 应用程序，它可以让你拥有的商店越多，或者使用 Shopify Plus，如果他们从现有的 Shopify 商店迁移，它可能会引入问题。

相比之下，Medusa 的无头抽象架构旨在支持任何类型的通道或设备。 您可以将任何类型的渠道（例如网站或应用程序）连接到您的 Medusa 服务器。 无论使用何种渠道，客户都将获得一致的体验和品牌标识。

此外，Medusa 提供了一个订单管理系统 (OMS)，旨在在一个地方管理您的所有订单，而不管它们被放置在哪个渠道上。 您可以从Medusa 管理员中完成、运送、退货和交换订单。

在 10 分钟内从 Shopify 迁移到美杜莎

许多企业坚持使用他们使用的平台，因为从一个平台转移到另一个平台很困难。 但是，如果您有兴趣从 Shopify 迁移到 Medusa，您可以使用我们的插件在 10 分钟内完成！

您可以查看本教程以了解如何快速从 Shopify 迁移到 Medusa 。

结论

有很多理由考虑美杜莎而不是 Shopify。 虽然 Shopify 提供了很多功能并且易于使用，但它不像 Medusa 那样可定制或灵活。

如果您正在寻找可定制且对开发人员友好的无头电子商务平台，Medusa 就是您的理想之选。

使用快速入门指南，您可以在几分钟内开始使用美杜莎。

如果您有任何与美杜莎相关的问题或疑问，请随时通过Discord与美杜莎团队联系。



原文:

Shopify is a hosted ecommerce platform that allows businesses to create online stores. Shopify is easy to use and has a lot of features, which is why it is so popular. However, it also has limitations for both businesses and developers that make it a hassle to work with as time passes.

Medusa is an open source headless commerce platform that is built on top of Node.js. Medusa is open source and is completely free to use. It has a growing community due to its simple architecture and extendibility.

This article explores some reasons why you should consider switching from Shopify to Medusa for your ecommerce stack.

Open Source Solutions are The Future

Enterprise solutions like Shopify take care of setting up the tech stack of businesses, which makes them appealing for people just getting started with ecommerce without developer resources. However, businesses soon enough realize that this limits them as they have no control or ownership of their stack. They have no freedom in choosing what services or infrastructure they want to use.

Open source solutions offer a great number of advantages for businesses, the most notable of which is the ability to completely own their tech stack. Businesses can have full access to the software and make any changes or additions that they want to it without having to wait for permission from the original developer. This lets businesses tailor the software exactly to their needs, making it a perfect fit for their business.

Open source solutions are often developed by large communities of developers, who are constantly working on adding new features and improving the software. This gives businesses a great advantage as their software is being constantly battle-tested and improved by a large community, rather than having to do it all manually with a smaller team.

This also means that businesses and their development teams can get help and support from the community whenever necessary. Medusa, for instance, has a Discord server where anyone can get help from the community and the core team of Medusa almost instantly.

More Customization Capabilities

Shopify has a lot of out-of-the-box features and is a beginner-friendly platform that people without technical knowledge can easily navigate. Shopify is a powerful platform but businesses often have specific use cases to cater to or need a tailored ecommerce experience. This leads businesses and developers to employ hacky workarounds which causes additional hassles and issues with time.

Medusa, on the other hand, is perfect for customization because its architecture is designed to be flexible and extendable. Medusa can easily be modified or customized specifically to suit each use case. For example, Medusa has a component-based structure that makes it easier to change one part of the structure without inferring with the rest, etc.

Because it is open source, developers can easily change Medusa’s core as they wish and build storefronts or admin panels on top of it using Medusa’s API.

Create Storefronts Tailored for Businesses

Storefronts are important for ecommerce businesses as they establish the brand’s identity and provide an interface for their customers to purchase their products.

There are a lot of Shopify themes and templates that provide businesses with limited customization capabilities. However, those customization capabilities are not enough for businesses to create a completely tailored experience based on their business. This becomes more of an issue when businesses offer custom products with a different purchase experience.

Headless commerce alternatives like Medusa, on the other hand, decouple the frontend from the backend. Developers have full freedom to customize the functionalities and design of the storefront or use whatever frontend framework they prefer without affecting the backend. All they need is to interact with the backend using REST APIs.

You can utilize these storefronts with your Medusa server: one built with Next.js, one built with Gatsby and another built with medusa.express. You can also create your storefront with the framework of your choice just by connecting to Medusa’s REST APIs. Your frontend development will be faster since updates to the frontend may be done without impacting the backend.

Better Developer Experience

Developers working with Shopify often end up building a whole suite of custom applications running on the side of Shopify to enable customizations. This makes it difficult to maintain over time and leads to high technical debt.

Compared to other headless ecommerce platforms which have complex infrastructures, Medusa’s architecture is simpler and easier. Everything, including its core engine and all the plugins, integrations, and third-party services runs in a single Node JS server making it easier for developers to incorporate plugins without using connectors and translation layers.

Additionally, businesses can rely on one developer to maintain Medusa because of how simple it is. The storefront, admin panel, and the backend all use JavaScript and Typescript. This makes it easy to switch between working on one or the other.

Also, As the frontend and the backend are decoupled, you don’t need developers who are skilled in both the frontend and backend. You can have one for each.

Moreover, Medusa provides excellent developer resources including rich documentation, a CLI that automates the redundant tasks developers face, and a React hooks library and a JavaScript client to easily interact with the backend while building a storefront for Medusa.

Developers can also report any issues they find while using Medusa, suggest new features, or open pull requests (PR) to fix issues they find on the GitHub repository.

Additionally, if developers run into any issues while using Medusa, they can get assistance from Medusa’s core team and community on their Discord server.

Multi-Currency Support

Multi-currency support is an essential feature that businesses can benefit a lot from in their ecommerce stores. It should allow them to sell in multiple currencies and have complete control over the pricing of products in different currencies.

Shopify only offers multi-currency support if you use Shopify Payments, which is just available in 17 countries. So, if your store is operating in a country that is not on the list of supported countries, the multi-currency feature would not work.

Even if a business can use Shopify’s multi-currency feature, they are not capable of managing the prices of products per currency unless they use the Advanced plan.

Businesses using Shopify often resort to creating multiple stores to manage the different currencies. However, that leads to a lot of manual work and human errors as they need to manage products, orders, and configurations in different stores.

With Medusa, businesses can choose an unlimited number of regions that they want to sell in, select what countries are a part of a region, what currency is used for each region, and set the price of a product for each currency all from one place.

This removes any limitation imposed on businesses and developers and the need for additional, redundant work. You can still manage all your products, orders, and store configuration from one place while supporting multiple currencies.

Suggested Read: How Medusa Resolves Shopify's Multi-Currency Issues

Omnichannel Support

Omnichannel support, in short, means a unified experience across channels. For example, a user can browse and purchase products with a similar experience whether they’re on the website or the mobile app.

Shopify does not support an omnichannel strategy. Businesses that want to utilize omnichannel will either need Shopify Apps, which can weigh down a store the more you have, or use Shopify Plus, which can introduce issues if they’re moving from an existing Shopify store.

In contrast, Medusa's headless and abstract architecture is designed to support any type of channel or device. You can connect any type of channel, such as websites or apps, to your Medusa server. Customers will have a consistent experience and brand identity no matter what channel they use.

Additionally, Medusa provides an order management system (OMS) that is designed to manage all of your orders in one place, regardless of the channel that they were placed on. You can fulfill, ship, return, and swap orders all from the Medusa admin.

Migrating from Shopify to Medusa in 10 minutes

A lot of businesses stick with the platform they use because moving from one platform to another is hard. However, if you’re interested in migrating from Shopify to Medusa, you can do that in 10 minutes using our plugin!

You can check out this tutorial to learn how you can quickly migrate from Shopify to Medusa.

Conclusion

There are many reasons to consider Medusa over Shopify. While Shopify offers a lot of features and is easy to use, it is not as customizable or flexible as Medusa.

Medusa provides developers and businesses with a simple architecture and extendibility with a lot of features. If you are looking for a headless ecommerce platform that is customizable and developer-friendly, Medusa is the platform for you.

You can get started with Medusa in minutes using the quickstart guide.

Should you have any issues or questions related to Medusa, then feel free to reach out to the Medusa team via Discord.
item.author: aldrichalvarez

item.title: 为什么我专注于 Next.js 网站开发 | Why I Specialize in Next.js Website Development
item.description:
如果您只是想知道 Next.js 是否是构建网站的好选择，那么答案肯定是肯定的。 我将告诉你所有关于 Next.js 与 Django 或 Java Spring 等其他框架的比较，以及为什么它在某些领域甚至可能更好。

多年来，我尝试了大量不同的网站开发框架，例如用 Python 编写的 Django 和 Flask，以及 Java Spring。

所有这些框架都缺少一些东西，我觉得我正在与它们的局限性作斗争。

在此期间，我非常喜欢使用 create-react-app 制作 React.js 网站。 效果很好。

直到没有。

如果您是 React 开发人员，您可能也遇到过同样的问题：

  • 客户端渲染不利于 SEO 和网站性能
  • 如果您正在处理 API 密钥、身份验证或私人信息等敏感数据，则需要创建单独的后端服务
  • 捆绑包尺寸很大

在此期间，我听说了 Next.js，乍一看，它似乎解决了所有这些问题。

是什么让 Next.js 与众不同

多种不同的渲染方法（静态、服务器端、客户端）

  • 带有 API 路由的后端功能
  • 直接在前端页面中使用服务器端代码
  • 令人难以置信的工具，让您的生活变得如此轻松

    • 因此，让我们深入研究一下，为什么这些原因让 Next.js 对我来说毫无疑问。

    专为 SEO 打造

    有两件事使 Next.js 成为 SEO 的杀手级网站框架。

    其令人难以置信的性能和多种不同的页面渲染方法。

    唯一的缺点是，如果您想对页面内容进行任何更改，您必须重建整个站点。

    一个拥有十到一百页的网站平均需要大约一到两分钟的时间来构建。

    在选择某种渲染方法之前，请始终计算这些风险和回报。 检查您有多少页面，平均多久更新一次，以及构建它们需要多长时间。

    对于经常更新的页面，另一种方法是使用服务器端呈现。

    这将导致页面在用户请求时按需构建。 这比提前静态渲染它需要更长的时间。 特别是如果您使用无服务器功能。

    如果您经常使用服务器端渲染，请考虑将您的网站托管在 DigitalOcean App Platform 这样的平台上，它不会像 Netlify 或 Vercel 这样的无服务器平台那样拖慢您的速度。

    核心网络生命力

    如果 SEO 对您的网站很重要，Google 的核心 Web Vital 指标已成为一个重要的考虑因素。

    值得庆幸的是，Next.js 在这方面表现得非常好，这要归功于其多种不同的渲染方法。

    基本的 React 网站存在一个主要问题。 所有用户都可以看到您的所有代码。

    这意味着您不能在代码中存储 API 密钥之类的秘密。 如果您的网站仅使用 React 构建，您将被迫构建一个单独的后端服务，以便您可以隐藏您的 API 密钥。 这显然会显着增加开发时间并使您的架构更加复杂。

    这使您可以更快、更安全地构建您的网站。

    另一个好处是您可以直接在前端页面中使用后端代码，而不会暴露任何机密数据。 这意味着您可以在网站页面中查询数据库或内容管理系统，从而完全跳过创建 API 路由。

    令人难以置信的工具

    开源社区已经为 Next.js 构建了一些令人难以置信的工具，可以快速轻松地构建一些常用功能。

    对于身份验证，有next-auth 。

    对于端到端类型安全 API，有tRPC 。

    对于 SEO，有next-seo 。

    这些工具是我在几乎所有网站项目中使用的一些我最喜欢的工具。

    除了这些，你当然可以使用所有的 React 工具。

    所有这些都很好，但它们如何转化为实际的业务成果？

    在我切换到 Next.js 后，我注意到我的客户项目花费的时间更少，更不容易出错，并且在 SEO 方面表现更好。

    这就是为什么当客户来向我寻求他们网站的帮助时，我通常最终会推荐 Next.js

    房地产网站

  • 博客
  • 登陆页面
  • 复杂的网络应用
  • 信息量大的网站
  • 电子商务网站

    • 我的一位客户 StartSteps Digital Education 在某些页面上获得了高达 600% 的性能提升。 在此处阅读整个案例研究。 由于其 SEO 提升，这最终增加了他们从搜索引擎获得的流量。

    关键要点

    如果 SEO 和网站性能对您的业务很重要，您应该考虑使用 Next.js。

    Next.js is a great solution especially for freelancers as it allows just a single person to take control of the whole website architecture saving tons of time and money. Next.js 是一个很好的解决方案，特别是对于自由职业者来说，因为它只允许一个人控制整个网站架构，从而节省大量时间和金钱。



    原文:

    If you are just wondering if Next.js is a good choice for building a website, the answer is definitely YES. I will be telling you all about how Next.js compares to other frameworks like Django or Java Spring and why it might even be better is certain areas.

    Over the years I have tried tons of different website development frameworks such as Django and Flask that are written in Python, and Java Spring.

    All of these frameworks lacked something and I felt like I was fighting against the limitations of them.

    During this time I was heavily into making React.js websites using create-react-app. It worked great.

    Until it didn't.

    If you are a react developer you have probably faced these same issues too:

    • Client side rendering is bad for SEO and website performance
    • You are required to create a separate backend service if you are dealing with sensitive data such as API keys, authentication or private information
    • Bundle sizes are huge

    During this time I heard about Next.js and from the first look it seemed to solve all of these issues.

    What makes Next.js different

    The main selling points of Next.js for me are:

    • Multiple different rendering methods (static, server-side, client-side)
    • Backend capabilities with API routes
    • Using server side code directly in your frontend pages
    • Incredible tools that will make your life so much easier

    So let's take a deep dive and see why these reasons made Next.js a no brainer to me.

    Built for SEO

    There are two things that make Next.js a killer website framework for SEO.

    Its incredible performance and multiple different page rendering methods.

    Next.js allows you to pre-render your pages before your users request them, gaining huge performance boost. The only downside is that if you want to make any changes to the page's content you have rebuild the whole site.

    An average website with ten to a hundred pages takes around one to two minutes to build.

    Always calculate these risks and rewards before you choose a certain rendering method. Check how many pages you have, how often they are going to be updated on average, and how long does building them take.

    An alternative for pages that update often is to use server-side rendering.

    This will cause the page to be built on demand whenever users request it. This takes a little longer than statically rendering it ahead of time. Especially if you are using serverless functions.

    If you are using server-side rendering a lot consider hosting your website on a platform like DigitalOcean App Platform which is not going to slow you down as much serverless platforms like Netlify or Vercel.

    Core Web Vitals

    Google's Core Web Vital metrics have become an important thing to consider if SEO is important for your website.

    Thankfully Next.js performs extremely well in this regard thanks to its multiple different render methods.

    API routes

    Basic React websites suffer from a major problem. All of your code will be visible to all users.

    This means that you cannot store secrets like API keys in your code. Most common use cases for API keys are fetching data from a database or a content management system, or submitting forms for example.

    If your website was built with React only you would be forced to built a separate back-end service just so you can keep your API keys hidden. This obviously increases development time significantly and makes your architecture more complex.

    Next.js solves this problem by allowing you to create API routes within your regular Next.js application. This allows you to build your website significantly faster and more securely.

    Another benefit is that you can use your backend code directly in your frontend pages without exposing any confidential data. This means that you can query your database or content management system within your website pages, allowing you to skip creating API routes altogether.

    Incredible tooling

    The open source community has built some incredible tools for Next.js that make building some common features fast and easy.

    For authentication there is next-auth.

    For end-to-end typesafe APIs there is tRPC.

    For SEO there is next-seo.

    These tools are some of my favorites which I use in almost all of my website projects.

    In addition to these you will of course have access to all React tools.

    The Next.js advantage

    All of these things are nice, but how do they translate to actual business results?

    After I switched to Next.js I noticed that my client projects took less time, were less error prone, and performed better in regards to SEO.

    That's why when clients come to ask me for help for their website I usually end up recommending Next.js

    Next.js is the perfect website framework for websites such as:

    • Real estate websites
    • Blogs
    • Landing pages
    • Complex web applications
    • Information heavy sites
    • eCommerce sites

    One of my clients, StartSteps Digital Education, got up-to 600 percent performance increase on certain pages. Read the whole case study here. This ended up increasing the amount of traffic they are getting from search engines thanks to its SEO boost.

    Key takeaways

    You should consider using Next.js if SEO and website performance are important for your business.

    Next.js is a great solution especially for freelancers as it allows just a single person to take control of the whole website architecture saving tons of time and money.
item.author: austinshelby

item.title: 只需 10 个步骤即可轻松有效地在 Heroku 上部署您的 DJANGO 项目的新技巧 | THE NEW TRICK TO EASILY AND EFFECTIVELY DEPLOY YOUR DJANGO PROJECT ON HEROKU IN JUST 10 STEPS
item.description:
奥萨亨鲁

发表于在尝试将我的 Django 项目投入生产的两个星期之后，我可以告诉你的事实是，部署 Django 项目没有可靠的方法，但我可以向你保证 95%明确遵循本指南中的所有步骤，您将成功部署您的项目。 另外，如果您遇到任何挑战，请随时发表评论。

本文分为两部分，第一阶段我将向您展示如何在 Heroku 上部署您的 Django 项目，第二阶段您将学习如何使用云文件存储服务（如 aws s3 bucket）来存储您上传的媒体文件，我还将解释本练习中使用的所有 git 命令，以防你对 git 完全陌生，说了这么多，让我们开始吧。

  • 第 1 步。您需要在这里创建一个 Heroku 帐户，然后在 Heroku 上创建一个应用程序。

  • 步骤 2. 下载并安装以下应用程序git和heroku cli ，如果您已经下载了这些应用程序，请跳过此步骤并继续下一步。

  • 步骤 3. 使用此代码从终端登录您的 Heroku 帐户heroku login

  • 第 4 步。现在您已成功登录帐户，您需要在您的 git 上创建一个存储库，我们将使用以下命令执行此操作。

接下来，键入git status - 检查 git 项目的当前状态是否已进行任何更改。

使用git status ，您可以查看对项目所做的所有更改。

  • 第 5 步。您需要 Heroku 通过在终端中输入以下行来知道要将项目推送到其服务器上的哪个位置
    Heroku git: remote -a 'name-of-your-app-that-was-created-on-the-heroku-server'

  • 一些库是在您创建应用程序时在本地安装的，因此我们需要将这些库也安装在 heroku 服务器上，或者至少让 heroku 知道这些库存在于其他库中，以便在 Heroku 服务器上有效地运行您的应用程序，现在输入以下代码。

pip freeze > requirements.txt

接下来，我们需要使用 pip 安装一些库，创建一些文件并编辑我们的settings.py文件，然后才能将我们的项目推送到 heroku 服务器

pip install gunicorn
pip install whitenoise

步骤 7. 安装这些库后，在您的根文件夹中创建一个名为Procfile的文件并输入以下代码行

web: gunicorn 'name-of-project.wsgi

注意。 步骤 8. 再次运行此代码pip freeze > requirements.txt将新安装的库添加到需求文件中。

  • 步骤 9. 接下来，我们需要对我们的settings.py文件进行一些调整
    寻找有

    • 这意味着任何域地址都可以在部署后轻松访问您的 django 项目，另一方面，如果我们指定一个地址说www.myproject.com这意味着您只能从这里访问这个应用程序给定域。

    接下来，查找MIDDLEWARE部分并在其中添加以下代码行

    whitenoise.middleware.WhiteNoiseMiddleware

    注意：确保将其添加到第二行，因为您需要在安全库之后渲染静态文件

    最后，在您的settings.py文件中，在您的静态部分添加以下代码行，以便您的静态文件可以正确呈现

    STATICFILES_DIRS = [os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'static')]
    STATIC_ROOT =os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'staticfiles')

    • 步骤 10. 最后我们只需要在终端上运行几行代码，然后最后将我们的代码推送到 heroku 服务器第一次运行

    python manage.py collectstatic git status

    混帐添加。


    git add . 包括我们迄今为止所做的所有更改。

    再次运行git status ，您会注意到我们的代码已添加，接下来我们需要使用git commit -m 'First commit'将我们的代码提交到 git

    瞧！

    您的项目已经部署成功，您可以通过访问链接查看它通常是这种格式

    'name-of-project'.herokuapp.com

    注意：那里的许多教程都使用 django_heroku 或 django_on_heroku，如果您在 2022 年尝试使用这些库，我有点确定您会遇到错误我真的不知道为什么会出现错误，但我能找到唯一切实的答案给出的是这些库不再被维护，因此不再与heroku服务器上的某些功能兼容。

    如果您仍然遇到任何困难，请随时与我联系。

    Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mahmoud EL-kariouny - May 1 5月1日



    原文:

    Osahenru

    Posted on May 20

    After, two solid weeks of trying to move my Django project on to production, one thing I can tell you for a fact is that there is no sure fire way of deploying Django projects, but I can give you a 95% assurance that if you explicitly follow all the steps in this guide you’ll successfully deploy your project. Also, feel free to drop a comment if you encounter any challenge.

    This article is in two parts, in the first phase I will show you how to deploy your Django projects on Heroku and in the second phase you’ll learn how to use cloud file storage services like aws s3 bucket for storing you media file uploads, I'll also be explaining all the git command used during this exercise incase you're entirely new to git, with that much said let’s dive in.

    • Step 1. You need to create a Heroku account Here and then create an app on Heroku.

    • Step 2. Download and install the following apps git and heroku cli, if you already have these downloaded then skip this step and proceed to the next step.

    • Step 3. Login into your Heroku account from terminal using this code heroku login

    • Step 4. Now that you've successfully logged into account, you need to create a repository on your git and we will do that using the following command.

    git init - Initializes/create a repository/folder.

    Next, type git status - To check the current status of your git project if any changes have been made.

    with git status you can see all the changes that have been made on your project.

    • Step 5. You need Heroku to know where you want to push your project on its server by inputting the following line in your terminal
      Heroku git: remote -a 'name-of-your-app-that-was-created-on-the-heroku-server'

    • Step 6. At this step we can choose to push but if we do you'll encounter some errors; some libraries were installed locally while you were creating your app and as such we need those libraries to also be installed on the heroku server or at least let heroku know those libraries exist in other to efficiently run your app on the Heroku server, now type the following code.

    pip freeze > requirements.txt

    Next, we need to install some libraries using pip, create a few files and edit our settings.py file before we can push our project to the heroku server

    pip install gunicorn
    pip install whitenoise

    Gunicorn: Acts as a middleman between your application and the web server’s application in GETing and POSTing requests
    Whitenoise: whitenosie helps in rendering your static files on the Heroku hosting server.

    • Step 7. After installing these libraries create a file in your root folder named Procfile and input the following line of code

    web: gunicorn 'name-of-project.wsgi

    NB. A Procfile does not have any file extension

    • Step 8. Run this code again pip freeze > requirements.txt to add your newly installed libraries to your requirements file.

    • Step 9. Next, we need to make some adjustments to our settings.py file
      Look for the line that has

    ALLOWED_HOST = [ ] and change it to

    ALLOWED_HOST = ['*']

    What this implies is that any domain address can easily access your django project once it’s been deployed, on the other hand if we specify an address say www.myproject.com this implies that you'll only be able to access this app only from this given domain.

    Next, look for the MIDDLEWARE section and add the following line of code there

    whitenoise.middleware.WhiteNoiseMiddleware

    NB: ensure it is added on the second line as you need your static files to be render just after the security libraries

    Lastly, on your settings.py file add the following lines of codes in your static section, so your static files can be properly rendered

    STATICFILES_DIRS = [os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'static')]
    STATIC_ROOT =os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'staticfiles')

    • Step 10. Lastly we just need to run a few line of codes on our terminal and then finally push our code to the heroku server first run

    python manage.py collectstatic git status

    git add .


    git add . includes all the changes we have made so far.

    run git status again and you'll notice our code has been added, next we need to commit our code to git with git commit -m 'First commit'

    git commit is giving git the sole right to make the changes to our codes and finally we push our code with.

    git push heroku master

    VOILA!!!

    Your project has been successfully deployed, you can view it by visiting the link usually in this format

    'name-of-project'.herokuapp.com

    NB: Many tutorials out there make use of the django_heroku or django_on_heroku, in 2022 if you try using these libraries I'm slightly sure you'll encounter an error I really don't have answers as to why but the only tangible answer I can give is that these libraries are no longer been maintained and as such are no longer compatible with some functionalities on the heroku server.

    If you still experience any difficulty please do not hesitate to contact me.

    Mahmoud EL-kariouny - May 1
item.author: osahenru

item.title: MASAKHANE 在 NLP 领域所做的惊人工作 | THE AMAZING WORKS DONE BY MASAKHANE IN NLP SPACE
item.description:
再次嗨，欢迎阅读这篇内容丰富的新文章，该文章介绍了一个很棒的社区，在为非洲人加强非洲语言的自然语言处理(NLP) 研究方面做着非常了不起的工作。

非洲有 2000 多种语言。 尽管如此，非洲语言只占自然语言处理(NLP) 中可用资源和出版物的一小部分。 这是由于多种因素造成的，包括缺乏政府和资金的关注、可发现性、缺乏社区、纯粹的语言复杂性、复制论文的困难以及没有比较技术的基准。

关于正汉

它旨在建立一个自然语言处理研究人员社区，连接并发展它，促进和分享进一步的研究，以实现语言保存、工具构建，并提高其全球知名度和相关性。

此外，建立一个社区，帮助发现分布式研究的最佳实践，以供其他新兴研究社区应用。

如果您热衷于基于 NLP 解决非洲挑战，Masakhane 可以无障碍、开放地获得第一次使用非洲语言的 NLP 实践经验。 使任何人都可以在自己选择的并行语料库上训练机器翻译模型，并在线共享结果。

它不需要学术先决条件来进行研究或为 Masakhane 社区做出贡献。

你猜怎么了？ 您对我如何与这个很棒的社区建立联系有疑问吗？

真棒图像| 土坯股票

想成为马萨哈尼人吗？ 不用担心，Masakhane 有他们活跃的 Slack 工作区和邮件列表组，您可以在其中找到所有手头的更新和研究。

作品由 Masakhane 完成

  1. 从不同角度将人们联系起来解决基于非洲语言的研究问题： Masakhane 社区的任何研究人员都很容易开始研究他/她可能喜欢的任何语言，仅仅因为母语使用者的支持就在那里，你不需要不想担心学习如何解决挑战的非洲语言。 我看到许多来自国外的人对斯瓦希里语研究感兴趣，并支持在 Masakhane 社区中以斯瓦希里语为母语的人完成他们的项目，这太棒了，可以帮助世界各地的人们了解不同的文化和语言。
  2. 非洲语言机器翻译： Masakhane 有一个开源在线网络，用于仅针对非洲语言的机器翻译服务。 如果您想为这个项目做出贡献，用您的语言训练模型或想与 Masakhane 合作和工作，请在https://github.com/dsfsi/masakhane-web中了解如何操作或联系任何 Masakhane网络贡献者。 非洲语言项目的机器翻译现在有 52 种非洲语言的基准，可以在 Masakhane 项目的Github 页面上看到。
  3. 非洲语言的语言模型： Masakhane 社区能够发表有关非洲语言模型的不同论文，这是打破非洲语言模型可用性障碍的惊人举措之一。 您可以在此处找到该出版物。
  4. 数据收集： Masakhane 社区完成的数据收集项目之一是东非文本和语音，旨在为来自乌干达、坦桑尼亚的资源匮乏的东非语言提供开放、可访问和高质量的文本和语音数据集和肯尼亚。 该项目专注于以下语言的数据：Luganda、Runyankore-Rukiga、Acholi、Swahili 以及乌干达和肯尼亚之间跨境的 Luhya Languages 的一个子集。 在此处阅读有关该项目的更多信息。
  5. 非洲名称的命名实体识别：该项目的名称为MasakhaNER 。 该项目侧重于信息提取和从信息检索中识别非洲人的姓名、地点和人物。 由于非洲语言的大多数现有 NER 数据集是 WikiNER，它们会自动注释并且非常嘈杂，因为非洲语言的文本质量没有得到验证。 只有少数非洲语言具有人工注释的 NER 数据集。 您可以在 Masakhane Github 页面上找到该项目。

你可以如何贡献？

  1. 训练模型：根据您想要贡献的研究问题，您可以作为 Masakhane 社区成员参与不同模型的训练。
  2. 文档：您可以选择成为帮助记录项目和编写正在进行的项目进度的成员。
  3. 分析：如果你真的喜欢对模型和数据进行分析，可以通过Masakhane参与分析非洲语言的数据集，解决各种问题。
  4. 数据：人们有兴趣使用新技术来提升自己的语言。 没有人很高兴看到他们的语言在各种平台上以错误的方式构建，这是您通过 Masakhane 社区中的数据收集活动做出贡献的机会，以增加研究人员、数据科学家、软件开发人员和其他人对数据的可访问性解决非洲挑战。
  5. 指导：您可以通过提供建议或帮助来为您喜欢或您有经验的语言调整模型做出贡献，并帮助人们入门。 这可以应用于希望对您的原籍语言进行研究的成员，因此为他们的工作提供支持和赞美是最佳实践解决非洲语言特定挑战的好方法。
  6. 计算基础设施：如果你有计算基础设施，并且你真的很喜欢支持 Masakhane 计划，但你没有时间专注于训练各种 NLP 模型，那么只需帮助社区提供基础设施，或者你可以捐赠。
  7. 头脑风暴：您可以参加每周会议，并根据主题提出建议、想法或建议。 此外，如果您的社区中存在可以通过 NLP 技术解决的现有挑战，请随时与 Masakhane 社区分享，以找到解决该挑战的最佳方法。

目前

研究机构正在大力投资 Masakhane，将其作为任何所需资源的中心平台，以在其作品中包含一种或多种非洲语言。 组织、公司和投资者也加入其中，与 Masakhane 成员合作开展几个以非洲语言为核心的项目。 现在投资 Masakhane 有很多好处，因为当我们谈论非洲语言的自然语言处理时，这是最热门的事情。

感谢您抽出宝贵时间阅读本文，希望能帮助您了解自然语言处理在非洲语言环境中的进展，以及如何参与为 Masakhane 社区发起的倡议做出贡献。

乔丹卡莱布—— 耶稣塞哈斯 - Sep 23 '20 20 年 9 月 23 日



原文:

Hi once again, welcome to this new informative article about an awesome community doing really amazing work on strengthening  Natural Language Processing (NLP) research in African languages, for Africans.

Africa has over 2000 languages. Despite this, African languages account for a tiny portion of available resources and publications in Natural Language Processing (NLP). This is due to multiple factors, including a lack of focus from government and funding, discoverability, a lack of community, sheer language complexity, difficulty in reproducing papers, and no benchmarks to compare techniques.

About Masakhane

Masakhane is a research effort originally for Machine translation focused on African languages that are open-source, continent-wide, distributed online. It aimed to build a community of Natural Language Processing researchers, connect and grow it, spurring and sharing further research to enable language preservation, tool building, and increasing its global visibility and relevance.

Masakhane pushing to build datasets and tools to facilitate Natural Language Processing in African languages and pose new research problems to enrich the NLP  research landscape.

Also, to build a community that will help to discover best practices for distributed research, to be applied by other emerging research communities.

The best about Masakhane, it uses smooth methodologies which don't require any experience of NLP to be connected with the community. If you are passionate about solving African challenges based on NLP, Masakhane is barrier-free, open access to first hands-on NLP experience with African languages. Enable anyone to train Machine translation models on a parallel corpus of their own choice and the results shared online.

It requires no academic prerequisites to conduct research or contribute to the Masakhane community.

Guess what? have a question in your mind about how I can connect with this awesome community?

Awesome images | Adobe Stock

Want to be Masakhanian? Don't worry, here you go, Masakhane has their active slack workspace and mailing list group, where you can find all the updates and research at hand.

Works are done by Masakhane

  1. Connecting people from different perspectives on solving research problems based on African language: It is simple for any researcher in the Masakhane community to start research on any language he/she could prefer simply because the support from the native language speaker is there, you don't want to worry about learning the African language that you what to solve a challenge with. I have seen many people from abroad interested in Swahili research and supported to complete their project by native Swahili speakers in the Masakhane community, this is awesome and helps people across the world to understand different cultures and languages.
  2. Machine Translation for African languages: Masakhane has an open-source online web for machine translation services for solely African languages. Masakhane Web is the platform that aims at hosting the already trained machine translation models from the Masakhane community and allows contributions from users to create new data for retraining and improving the models. If you would like to contribute to this project, train a model in your language or want to collaborate and work with Masakhane, find out how in https://github.com/dsfsi/masakhane-web or reach out to any of the Masakhane Web contributors. The machine translation for the African language project now has 52 African languages with benchmarks which can be seen on the Masakhane project's Github page.
  3. Language models for African Languages: The Masakhane community is able to publish different papers about African language modeling, this is among amazing initiatives on breaking the barrier of African language model availability. You can find the publication here.
  4. Data Gathering: One of the data collection projects done by the Masakhane community is Text & Speech for East Africa, which was aimed to deliver open, accessible, and high-quality text and speech datasets for low-resourced East African languages from Uganda, Tanzania, and Kenya. The project was focused on data for the languages: Luganda, Runyankore-Rukiga, Acholi, Swahili, and a subset of Luhya Languages which are cross-border between Uganda and Kenya. Read more about the project here.
  5. Named Entity Recognition for African Names: The project goes with the name MasakhaNER . The project focused on information extraction and identifying African names, places, and people from information retrieval. Since the majority of existing NER datasets for African languages are WikiNER which are automatically annotated and are very noisy since the text quality for African languages is not verified. Only a few African languages have human-annotated NER datasets. You can find the project on the Masakhane Github page.

How you can contribute?

  1. Training a model: depending on the research problem you want to contribute you can just take part in training different models as a Masakhane community member.
  2. Documentation: You can opt to be part of members that help in documenting projects and writing progress about ongoing projects.
  3. Analysis: If you really like to perform analysis of models and data, through Masakhane you can take part in analyzing datasets of African languages to solve various problems.
  4. Data: People are interested in using new technology to advance their own languages. No one is happy seeing their languages are framed in a wrong way on various platforms, this is a chance for you to contribute through data collection activities in the Masakhane community to increase the accessibility of data for researchers, data scientists, software developers, and others to solve African challenges.
  5. Mentorship: You can contribute by providing advice or help to tune models for the languages you prefer or you had experience with and help people get started. This can be applied to members that want to apply research on the language of your origin, so providing support and compliment to their work is the great way to make the best practice of addressing specific challenges of the African language.
  6. Computation infrastructure: If you have computing infrastructures and your really love to support Masakhane initiatives but you don't have time to focus on training kinds of NLP models, just help the community with the infrastructures or you can just donate.
  7. Brainstorm: You can just join weekly meetings and contribute with suggestions, ideas, or advice depending on the topic. Also if you have an existing challenge within your community that can be solved by NLP technologies feel free to share with the Masakhane community to find the best way of solving that challenge.

Currently

Masakhane is gaining momentum in the world right now. Research institutions are heavily investing in Masakhane as a central platform for any needed resources to include one or more African languages in their works. Masakhane has many research collaborations with Google, Facebook as well as institutions in Africa. Organizations, companies, and investors are joining, too, by working together with Masakhane members on several projects which are at the core of African languages. There is much to gain from investing in Masakhane right now, as it is the hottest thing when we talk about natural language processing for African languages.

Thank you for taking your time to read this article, hope helps you to understand the progress of Natural language processing in the African language context and how you can take part in contributing to the initiatives started by the Masakhane community.

amananandrai - Oct 20 '20

Jordan Kalebu - Oct 1 '20

Jesús Seijas - Sep 23 '20
item.author: tonyloyt

item.title: 调查 EF Core 6.0 编译模型功能的性能优势 | Investigating the performance benefits of EF Core 6.0 compiled models feature
item.description:
随着微软去年发布的 Entity Framework Core 6.0 的第五个预览版，编译模型首次被引入。 这里的重点是使用此功能加快冷启动时间。

这将提供更好的启动性能，以及访问模型时通常更好的性能。” 本文将更详细地探讨此功能 - 它在 EF Core 中的适用位置、工作原理以及它提供的优势。 我们将通过我们自己的调查评估这些好处，该调查利用我们现有项目之一的数据库。 有问题的数据库包含许多实体、表和关系，使其成为测试编译模型有效性的完美用例。

该框架是对 ADO.NET 的增强，它为开发人员提供了一种在数据库中访问和存储数据的自动化方式。

被描述为“开源、轻量级和可扩展”的 EF Core 与其前身的主要区别在于，此更新版本兼容 Linux 和 OSX 以及 Windows。

该框架适合您的域和数据库，提供称为 DbContext 的功能。 此功能表示与数据库的连接，可用于查询和保存实体的实例； 它充当域模型和数据库之间的链接，允许开发人员流畅地配置映射的工作方式。

过去，所有映射都必须手动完成——这是一个及时而冗长的过程。 为了从数据库中读取数据，开发人员必须创建一个连接，退出计算机命令，然后记得关闭连接。 今天，随着 ORM 的引入，这不再是必要的，因此软件开发团队可以提高效率。

您可以使用 Dbcontext 添加、修改和删除数据。 通过这样做，您可以确保正在更新或删除的记录仍然包含与进程开始时相同的值。

来自 Web Framework Benchmarks 的数据显示，EF Core 6.0 现在的执行速度比其前身快 70% ，这进一步凸显了此最新更新中的全面改进。

当您处理具有许多属性和关系的大型模型时，此功能特别有用。

最初，EF Core 会询问数据库以更详细地了解这些关系。 编译模型使您能够在开发过程中静态地执行此过程。 使用一个非常简单的命令，您可以分析您的模型并生成降低冷启动速度的代码。

dotnet ef dbcontext optimize -output-dir MyCompiledModels --namespace MyCompiledModels

此命令将允许您优化 DbContext 并生成编译模型。 当 EF Core 编译模型时，它会找到您的配置并在您的 DbContext 中创建一个实例。 从该实例生成模型，然后使用该模型生成编译后的模型代码。

此命令的工作方式与添加和生成迁移时类似。 因此，Microsoft 建议您在更改模型时刷新模型。

从这里，我们可以告诉我们的代码我们想要使用我们的模型和这个函数：

options.UseModel(MyCompiledModels.BlogsContextModel.Instance);

现在我们对如何设置编译模型有了一些了解，让我们评估一下这个功能可以带来的一些好处。

对于许多使用合理大小模型的用户来说，编译模型不一定提供任何重大改进。 但是，如果您正在构建模型并努力执行您的第一个查询，那么编译模型提供了减少应用程序冷启动时间的巨大优势——随着模型变得更大，这一优势将保持不变。

相同的评估适用于模型大小。 这里的功能更适合具有大量实体类型、属性和关系的模型，否则这些模型会运行得很慢。 例如，Microsoft 对具有 449 个实体类型、6,390 个属性和 720 个关系的模型进行了调查，他们报告使用编译模型将启动时间加快了 10 倍（见下图）。

图片来自微软

使用编译模型还提供了您不必在开发过程中不断更改模型的优势。 如果模型保持不变，您可以在不编译的情况下运行应用程序——在循环时间方面可以节省大量时间。

然而，这一点也暗示了编译模型的缺点。 每次您的模型更改时，您都需要手动同步它。

所以编译好的模型不一定对每个人都有用，但是对于那些大的大模型来说，它保证了冷启动时间的巨大好处。 为了对此进行测试，本文的下一部分将调查来自 Audacia 现有项目之一的数据库——一个包含数百个表和关系的数据库。

此处的目的是研究将现有项目升级到 EF Core 6 并使用编译模型对具有大型架构的项目的性能优势。

三、调查

为了为案例研究选择合适的项目，我们需要找到一个具有足够大的 DbContext 的项目，该项目已经在 .NET 6 上运行。我们选择了一些满足这些标准的项目，并发现在建立了更多实体和关系的项目。 这导致您认为架构越大，您从该功能中获得的好处就越多。

选择的项目是一个拥有 300 多个实体的项目，其中绝大多数都对应于它自己的 SQL 表。

按照第 2 部分中概述的步骤，我们在该项目上启用了编译模型，并准备好测试影响。

示例应用程序

编写少量代码来评估这是否提高了启动性能很容易。 使用下面的代码，我们在解决方案中设置了一个独立的控制台应用程序，它执行以下操作：

  1. 为包含上述实体的上下文创建一个DbContextOptionsBuilder 。
  2. 告诉构建器使用optimize命令生成的编译模型。
  3. 实例化上下文。
 Console . WriteLine ( "Starting application" ); for ( var i = 0 ; i < 5 ; i ++) {   var stopWatch = new Stopwatch ();   stopWatch . Start ();   var builder = new DbContextOptionsBuilder < DatabaseContext >()           . UseSqlServer ( "***" )           . UseModel ( DatabaseContextModel . Instance );   using var context = new DatabaseContext ( builder . Options )   Console . WriteLine ( $" { nameof ( DatabaseContext )} instantiated after { stopWatch . Elapsed : c } ");   stopWatch . Stop (); }

结果

为了测试差异，我们在创建编译模型之前运行了这个程序，这意味着省略.UseModel(DatabaseContextModel.Intance) 。 这产生了以下结果：

 Starting application DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:02.1330204 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0006707 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0001553 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0002751 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0000814

如您所见，第一次实例化它比后续迭代花费的时间要长得多。 这展示了冷启动（第一次实例化）和热启动（后续实例化）之间的区别。 这是 EF Core DbContext 首次启动的典型情况，也是编译模型旨在减少的情况。

现在，设置编译模型并告诉构建器使用它会导致以下结果：

 Starting application DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.4312566 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0070515 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0001587 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0000955 DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0000988

因此，我们将启动时间缩短了四倍，从超过两秒减少到不到半秒。 看到这一点在我们的一个项目中得到实践是令人鼓舞的。 我们希望这只会在更多实体和关系添加到 DbContext 时得到改善。

因此，了解应用程序何时/如何启动非常重要。 例如，如果您在应用程序自动关闭的无服务器架构中采用某种基于消耗的计划，您可能会发现您的应用程序在每次调用时都会进行冷启动。 因此，减少此启动时间可能会带来巨大的经济利益，因为它会花费更少的时间来实例化上下文。

对于在单个 DbContext 中包含大量信息的遗留系统来说，这将是一个非常有用的工具。 但是，有人可能会提出这样的论点，即一旦您拥有包含这么多实体的 DbContext，初始启动时间等问题就不是您主要关心的问题，因为在单个数据存储中拥有这么多信息会带来风险。

在 Audacia，我们一直在寻找可以改进新的和现有应用程序的方法。 诸如编译模型之类的功能将极大地激励大型应用程序升级到 .NET 6，并表明 Microsoft 对持续改进的兴趣。

致谢

本文由Jack Gill撰写，并与首席顾问Owen Lacey合作制作，后者为调查提供了专家见解​​。



原文:

With Microsoft's fifth preview of Entity Framework Core 6.0 released last year, Compiled Models were introduced for the first time. The focus, here, was to speed up cold startup times using this feature.

“Compiled models will allow the generation of a compiled form of the EF model. This will provide both better startup performance, as well as generally better performance when accessing the model.” — Plan for Entity Framework Core 6.0

This article will explore this feature in more detail - where it fits in EF Core, how it works and the benefits it offers. We will evaluate these benefits via our own investigation, which draws on a database from one of our existing projects. The database in question contains many entities, tables and relationships, making it a perfect use case for testing out the effectiveness of compiled models.

1. EF Core 6.0

EF Core is an Object/Relational Mapping (O/RM) framework for .NET applications that developers use to access a database. The framework is an enhancement to ADO.NET that provides developers with an automated way to access and store data in a database.

Described as being “open-source, lightweight and extensible”, the main difference between EF Core and its predecessor is that this updated version is compatible across Linux and OSX as well as Windows.

This framework fits between your domain and database, providing a feature called a DbContext. This feature represents a connection to a database and can be used to query and save instances of your entities; it acts as a link between the domain model and the database, allowing developers to fluidly configure how mappings work.

Entity Framework is responsible for opening a connection to the database, and can subsequently perform read/write actions on the dataset via interacting with items in a given DbSet.

It used to be that all mapping had to be done manually - a timely and drawn out process. In order to read data from the database, a developer would have to create a connection, exit the computer command, and then remember to close the connection. Today, with the introduction of ORM, this is no longer necessary, so software development teams can with greater efficiency.

1.1. EF Core 6.0 Features

The EF Core framework offers many additional advantages, such as the ability to write strongly typed LINQ queries as well as work with data using objects of domain-specific classes instead of the underlying database tables and columns (where this data is stored).

You can add, modify and remove data using your Dbcontext. The SaveChanges method allows you to commit any changes made to your data.

Asynchronous saving is also provided in EF Core with the SaveChangesAsync() method, which prevents threads from being interrupted whilst changes are being written to the database.

Optimistic Concurrency is also defaulted to prevent overwriting of changes made by another user after data is retrieved from the database. By doing so, you are ensuring that the record being updated or deleted still contains the same values as it did when the process started.

Data from Web Framework Benchmarks shows that EF Core 6.0 now performs 70% faster than its predecessor, which further highlights the all round improvements in this latest update.

2. Compiled Models

Compiled Models is a feature that has been introduced in version 5.0 with the intention of improving cold startup times for models. This feature is particularly useful when you are dealing with a large model with many properties and relationships.

Initially, EF Core interrogates the database to understand these relationships in more detail. Compiled Models enable you to carry out this process statically during development. Using a very simple command, you can analyse your model and generate code that reduces cold startup speed.

dotnet ef dbcontext optimize -output-dir MyCompiledModels --namespace MyCompiledModels

This command will allow you to optimise the DbContext and generate a Compiled Model. When EF Core compiles the model, it finds your configuration and creates an instance in your DbContext. A model is generated from this instance, and the compiled model code is then generated using this model.

This command works in a similar way to when you’re adding and generating a migration. For this reason, Microsoft advises that the model is refreshed whenever your model is changed.

From here, we can tell our code that we want to use our model with this function:

options.UseModel(MyCompiledModels.BlogsContextModel.Instance);

Now that we have some idea of how to get set up with Compiled Models, let's evaluate some of the benefits that this feature can bring.

2.2 Compiled Models Benefits

For many users operating with a reasonable size model, Compiled Models don’t necessarily offer any significant improvements. However, if you are in the process of building your model and working toward executing your first query, then Compiled Models offer the great advantage of reducing cold startup times for your application — a benefit that remains constant as your model grows bigger.

The same evaluation applies to Model Size. Functionality here is more suited to models with lots of entity types, properties, and relationships which would otherwise operate very slowly. For example, Microsoft conducted an investigation on a model with 449 entity types, 6,390 properties and 720 relationships, where they reported 10x faster startup times using Compiled Models (see image below).

Image from Microsoft

Using Compiled Models also offers the advantage that you don't have to constantly change the model during development. Providing the model stays the same, you can run the application without compiling it — a great timesaver when it comes to loop time.

However, this point also alludes to a disadvantage with Compiled Models; each time your model changes, you need to manually synchronise it.

So compiled models are not necessarily useful for everyone, but for those big big models, it promises great benefits for cold startup time. To put this to the test, the next part of this article will investigate a database from one of Audacia’s existing projects — a database with several hundred tables and relationships.

The intention, here, is to investigate the performance benefit of upgrading an existing project to EF Core 6 & using compiled models, for a project with a large schema.

3. Investigation

In order to select an appropriate project for a case study, we needed to find a project with a large enough DbContext, which is already running on .NET 6. We selected a few projects that fulfil these criteria, and found a marked improvement on the projects with more entities & relationships set up. This leads you to think that the larger your schema, the more benefit you would get from this feature.

The selected project was one with over 300 entities, the vast majority of which correspond to its own table in SQL.

By following the steps outlined in part 2, we enabled compiled models on this project and were ready to test the impact.

3.1. Example Application

It was easy enough to write a small amount of code to assess whether this improves start up performance. With the below code, we set up a stand-alone console application in the the solution, which does the following:

  1. Creates a DbContextOptionsBuilder for the context which contains the entities mentioned above.
  2. Tells the builder to use the compiled model generated by the optimize command.
  3. Instantiate the context. 
Console.WriteLine("Starting application"); for (var i = 0; i < 5; i++)
{
   var stopWatch = new Stopwatch();
   stopWatch.Start();
   var builder = new DbContextOptionsBuilder<DatabaseContext>()
           .UseSqlServer("***")
           .UseModel(DatabaseContextModel.Instance);    using var context = new DatabaseContext(builder.Options)
   Console.WriteLine($"{nameof(DatabaseContext)} instantiated after
 {stopWatch.Elapsed:c}");
    stopWatch.Stop();
}

3.2. The Results

In order to test the difference, we ran this program before we created the compiled model, which meant omitting .UseModel(DatabaseContextModel.Intance). This produced the following results:

Starting application

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:02.1330204

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0006707

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0001553

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0002751

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0000814

As you can see, the first time it’s instantiated takes far longer than the subsequent iterations. This demonstrates the difference between a cold start (first instantiation) and a warm start (subsequent instantiations). This is typical of the first time an EF Core DbContext starts up, and is something that Compiled Models aims to reduce.

Now, setting up Compiled Models and telling the builder to use it results in the following:

Starting application

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.4312566

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0070515

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0001587

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0000955

DatabaseContext instantiated after 00:00:00.0000988

We’ve therefore reduced our startup time fourfold, from over two seconds to under half a second. It’s encouraging to see this working in practice on one of our projects. We would expect this to only improve should more entities and relationships be added to the DbContext.

As a result of this, having knowledge of when/how your application starts up can be really important. For example, if you’re on some sort of consumption-based plan in a serverless architecture in which applications shut down automatically, you might find that your application does a cold start each time it’s invoked. Reducing this startup time could therefore have a large financial benefit as it would spend less time instantiating the context.

4. Conclusion

Compiled Models is a new EF Core 6 feature which vastly improves the startup times of DbContexts with large schemas. This would be a very useful tool for legacy systems with a lot of information in a single DbContext. However, one could make the argument that once you have a DbContext with this number of entities, problems like initial start up time wouldn’t be your main concern, as having that amount of information in a single data store presents its own risks.

At Audacia, we’re constantly looking at ways we can improve our new and existing applications. Features like Compiled Models would be a great incentive for a large application to upgrade to .NET 6, and demonstrates Microsoft’s appetite for continuous improvements.

Acknowledgements

This article was written by Jack Gill and produced in collaboration with Principal Consultant Owen Lacey who provided his expert insight for the investigation.
item.author: audaciasoftware

item.title: 致有抱负的数据科学家的话 | WORDS TO ASPIRING DATA SCIENTISTS
item.description:
充满热情是一回事，有勇气去做自己热爱的事情是一种巨大的胜利。

您是否曾经梦想过有一天您能够在 ABC 公司使用数据科学技术解决业务问题，而所有利益相关者都在等待您的分析得出的见解以得出他们的决定？

如果没有，只需花几秒钟时间考虑一下那一刻。 如果您热爱自己作为数据爱好者所做的事情，那将是一个了不起的时刻。

但是等等，你有没有用数据科学解决过任何挑战？ 如果是，您的工作是否可以与利益相关者分享并回答所提出的问题？

抱歉，现在您对您从事的项目有很多疑问。 听着停止为你自己解决问题，做真实的自己，解决社区问题。

职业发展的过程并不容易，它需要一个人努力成为他们专业中的佼佼者。 今天，我将与有抱负的数据科学家讨论他们如何在不被雇用的情况下巩固自己在该领域的技能，这可以帮助您在任何公司获得第一份工作。

作为一名有抱负的数据科学家，可以巩固你的技能：

  1. 像数据科学家一样思考和行动。
  2. 与社区分享您正在做的事情。

以数据科学家的身份思考和行动：

您是否了解真正的数据科学家在日常工作中在做什么？如果没有，您可以使用任何社交媒体平台与各种专业数据科学家联系并提出一些问题 大多数人通过专注于构建高性能模型来开始他们的学习之旅使用 AB 算法，我曾在不同的数据科学竞赛平台上看到人们构建模型，但在比赛结束后，他们甚至没有记录他们的解决方案。

甚至不要自责我以前曾经这样做过，只需将其添加到您的待办事项列表中即可。

通过构建端到端的解决方案，抓住每一个机会来展示和表现你作为一个真正的数据科学家为某家商业公司工作。 我之所以提到这一点，是因为没有人愿意聘请数据科学家来试用他们的业务，雇主想要能够解决业务挑战的人。

考虑到你的第一份工作不会在谷歌、Facebook、亚马逊、苹果或 Netflix 等大公司工作。 谈到这些公司，他们的数据基础设施已经非常发达，可以说已经在数据驱动的文化中，所以他们拥有能够从事数据操作和 ETL 管道工作的数据工程师，以确保数据准备好进行分析，而数据科学家、数据分析师、商业智能分析师和商业分析师可以从组织良好的数据中获得洞察力并开发报告。

让我们进入现实，稍微扫描一下你周围的人，尤其是在非洲，有多少数据工程师在两个或多个大城市周围的某些公司工作？

希望你明白我的意思，所以你应该考虑如果你在那些公司工作，你将从事从不同来源提取和收集数据、组织数据、执行分析等工作。 有时甚至自己构建数据基础设施。作为数据科学家应对业务挑战时的最佳实践：

  1. 将业务挑战视为数据科学挑战

问题框架是数据科学过程中至关重要且经常被忽视的一步，但如果不了解利益相关者作为数据科学家的真实需求，我真的无法构建正确和最佳的解决方案。 更重要的是退后一步，仔细看看我们想要解决的问题。数据框架就是定义你想要解决的问题，让我们看一个现实世界的例子：

利益相关者观点：

让我们假设您是 ABC 公司的数据科学家，然后您接到某个银行经理的电话，经过一番来回，解释说他们有拖欠贷款的问题，他们需要一个预测贷款的程序将来会默认。 不幸的是，他现在必须结束通话，但他稍后会赶上你。 与此同时，你开始理解这个问题。 数据科学观点：

虽然经理很清楚他为你提供了所有必要的信息，但你靠在椅子上，重述问题：

  • 今天，银行借钱给客户。 客户向银行承诺在接下来的几个月/几年内逐步偿还贷款。 不幸的是，一些客户无法做到这一点，并将拖欠贷款。
  • 到目前为止一切都很好。 银行会给你过去的数据，并指示你做出预测。 很公平，但是有什么具体可以再次预测的呢？
  • 他们是否需要知道每笔贷款是否会违约？
  • 他们是否更关心整个银行的违约趋势？
  • 问题的性质可以作为分类或回归挑战来解决。
  • 在分类挑战银行贷款的情况下，可以分为两组：贷款违约和另一组仍在执行的贷款。 在回归挑战的情况下，可能是在给定时间内违约的贷款百分比或银行在给定月份将损失的总金额。

然后在与利益相关者开会时等待听取他们的意见，并与他们分享您作为数据科学家所拥有的一切。 然后从中，您将获得通过匹配银行要求来有效解决问题的技术。

这部分将有助于为上述问题选择最佳数据集。

从此处了解有关应对业务挑战的更多信息

最好的部分是通过使用统计技术和可视化工具进行分析来了解数据集。 它有助于确定如何最好地操纵数据源以获得所需的答案，从而使数据科学家更容易发现模式、发现异常、检验假设或检查假设。作为通过 EDA 的数据科学家，您可以发现缺失值，特征不一致、条目重复等等接下来的任务就是清理你从各个角度观察到的数据，这可以通过解决数据发现的所有问题来完成。你可以从这里了解更多关于 EDA 的信息。

特征工程是指在使用机器学习或统计建模创建预测模型时，使用领域知识从原始数据中选择和转换最相关变量的过程。

从现在从 EDA 部分获得的见解来看，是时候通过生成新特征和转换特征、特征提取以及从给定数据集中选择最佳特征来找到合并某些概念的最佳方法了。从此处了解有关特征工程的更多信息.

建模过程意味着训练机器学习算法来预测所选特征的标签，根据业务需求对其进行调整，并在保留数据上对其进行验证。

最好的选择是从简单的算法开始，例如线性回归和逻辑回归，考虑到没有一种机器学习算法最适合每个数据科学问题，因此从了解您正在处理的挑战开始，选择最佳算法将很顺利算法。 但不要忘记简单的解决方案是最好的解决方案。

然后，您应该在训练后评估模型，以使用各种机器学习评估指标了解它们的性能。 这里的最佳实践是使用多个评估指标来检查模型性能。从许多选项中选择了最佳模型之后，是时候深入了解最终模型预测的理解了，在这里你应该使用机器学习模型解释工具来检查您的模型，它将在公平、信任、可靠性和因果关系等几个方面帮助您的解决方案。从此处了解有关机器学习模型解释的更多信息

在这里，您正在尝试准备最好的东西与利益相关者分享，简单，因为您无法向他们展示代码，只需设计一个简单且组织良好的报告，列出您从数据集中获得的见解、建议并与他们分享。

该报告应包括您处理的数据集的结论。

在整个数据科学之旅中理解问题的重要性是值得的，因为您无法从您甚至不会理解或向利益相关者解释的解决方案中得出结论。 您应该能够说出机器学习模型预测的内容以及在何种情况下您构建的模型无法正常工作的程度。

考虑记录您正在进行的每个项目。

与社区分享您正在做的事情：

在构建端到端解决方案后，只需考虑记录您的解决方案并与社区分享，专家将看到您的作品并提供他们的反馈，这将帮助您成长。

但有时在互联网上，你不会想到谁会被你的作品所吸引并有兴趣与你合作。 此外，它将帮助其他人向您学习并提高他们的理解。

您可以在 LinkedIn 或 Twitter 上联系经验丰富的数据科学家，请他们审查您的工作，您甚至可以要求设置电话来讨论您的工作，这将为您提供正确的方向，以改进您通过数据科学应对业务挑战的方式技术。

当您要求为 ABC 公司工作时，这对您来说并不难，因为您有经验和曝光度。

成为一名数据科学家不仅仅是构建模型，还有很多事情需要考虑，例如业务领域理解、讲故事、构建端到端的数据科学解决方案等等。

每次您参加数据科学竞赛或黑客马拉松时，只需专注于构建简单且最佳的解决方案，以解决利益相关者希望听到的业务问题。 在黑客马拉松结束时，只需花几天时间记录您的工作并与社区分享。 将自己置于该公司唯一的数据科学家的位置，您将需要提供最佳解决方案，用于优化其产品的价值。

创建数据科学解决方案并不是从模型构建开始和结束的。 如果你是一名有抱负的数据科学家，我建议你在你想从事的行业中获得一些领域知识。

谢谢你，如果你觉得这篇文章有趣且内容丰富，请不要犹豫与他人分享，分享是关怀。



原文:

Being passionate is one thing, the courage of doing what you are passionate for is a great win.

Had you ever dreamed of the day you will be capable of solving business problems using data science technology on ABC company, while all stakeholders are waiting for the insights from your analysis to conclude their decisions?

If not, just take a few seconds to think about that moment. It is an amazing moment if you love what you do as a data enthusiast.

But wait, have you solved any challenges using data science? If yes does your work can be shared with stakeholders and answer the question stated?

Sorry, now you have a lot of questions in your mind about the projects you have worked on. Listen stop solving problems for you're own just be real and solve community problems.

The process of career growth is not easy it requires a person to struggle to become the best in their professionals. Today I will be talking to aspiring data scientists on how they can solidify their skills in this field without being employed, this can help you to secure your first job at any company.

Things to solidify you are skills as an aspiring data scientist:

  1. Think and Act as a data scientist.
  2. Share what you are doing with the community.

Think and Act as a data scientist:

Have you figured out what real data scientists are doing in their day to day work, if not you can just use any social media platform to connect with various professional data scientists and ask some questions Majorities start their learning journey by focusing on building high performing models using AB algorithms, I have been seen in different data science competition platforms people building models but after the competitions, they just don’t even document their solutions.

Don't even blem yourself i used to do that before, just add it to your to-do list.

Take every chance you get to show and perform you are working as a real data scientist for a certain business company by building end-to-end solutions. I’m mentioning this because no one wants to hire a data scientist for trial on their business, employers want people who are capable of solving business challenges.

Take into consideration that your first job will not be on giant companies like Google, Facebook, Amazon, Apple, or Netflix.
